name: Drinks
description: Beverages, cocktails, beer, wine, spirits, and soft drinks

questions.q: Absolut is a well-known brand of which spirit?

source.notes: Made in Åhus, Sweden, since 1879.

questions.q: Sake is a traditional fermented rice beverage originating in which country?

source.notes: Sake is officially called 'nihonshu' in Japanese.

questions.q: Starbucks is primarily known for selling what beverage?

source.notes: Founded 1971 in Seattle, Washington.

questions.q: Scotch is a type of what alcoholic spirit?

source.notes: Scotch whisky must be distilled and aged in Scotland for at least three years.

questions.q: Schweppes is famous for producing which kind of bubbly mixer?

source.notes: Founded 1783 by Johann Schweppe in Geneva, Switzerland.

questions.q: Tequila is distilled from which Mexican plant?

source.notes: Specifically the blue Weber agave.

questions.q: Guinness, the famous stout beer, originated in which country?

source.notes: Brewed at St. James's Gate in Dublin since 1759.

questions.q: Which 19th-century pharmacist invented Coca-Cola in 1886?

source.notes: Atlanta pharmacist; sold the formula to Asa Candler in 1888.

questions.q: The beer Stella Artois is brewed primarily in which country?

source.notes: Brewed in Leuven since 1926.

questions.q: The lager brand Foster's is marketed globally as being from which country?

source.notes: Founded in Melbourne in 1887; now owned by Asahi.

questions.q: What is the name of the iconic bright-orange soft drink nicknamed 'Scotland's other national drink'?

source.notes: Produced by A.G. Barr since 1901.

questions.q: Yorkshire Tea is produced in which English spa town?

source.notes: Produced by Taylors of Harrogate since 1977.

questions.q: Oolong is a type of what beverage originating from China?

source.notes: Semi-oxidized tea — between green and black on the oxidation scale.

questions.q: Dr Pepper was invented in Waco, Texas, in which decade?

source.notes: Created by pharmacist Charles Alderton in 1885.

questions.q: WKD, a popular UK alcopop, uses which spirit as its base?

source.notes: Originally from Beverage Brands UK; launched 1996.

questions.q: The fruit juice brand Tropicana is owned by which food-and-beverage giant?

source.notes: Acquired by PepsiCo in 1998; partial sale to PAI Partners in 2022.

questions.q: Volvic natural mineral water comes from which European country?

source.notes: Bottled from volcanic springs in the Auvergne region.

questions.q: By EU law, a sparkling wine can only be called 'Champagne' if produced in which French region?

source.notes: Granted UNESCO World Heritage status in 2015.

questions.q: Cognac is a protected-origin type of which distilled spirit?

source.notes: Distilled from white wine grapes in the Cognac region of southwestern France.

questions.q: Baileys Irish Cream is produced in which country?

source.notes: Created in Dublin in 1974; a blend of Irish whiskey and cream.

questions.q: Red Bull the energy-drink company, as we know it globally today, was launched from which country in 1987?

source.notes: Adapted by Austrian Dietrich Mateschitz from Thailand's 'Krating Daeng'.

questions.q: Carlsberg, one of the world's largest brewers, is from which country?

source.notes: Founded in Copenhagen in 1847 by J.C. Jacobsen.
